title: Outdoor / field-science guides (weather, ecology, astronomy-by-eye)
context: oer-science-labs primarily covers indoor, household-materials experiments. Many schools and informal educators also have access to outdoor spaces — schoolyards, parks, gardens — where observation-based science (weather measurement, ecology transects, astronomy-by-eye) is safe, low-cost, and highly engaging. Outdoor guides introduce additional safety considerations not present indoors: sun exposure, heat, UV, environmental hazards (uneven terrain, insects, plants), and weather-dependent timing. These must be addressed as first-class safety concerns in the guide schema.
objective: Author a set of outdoor and field-science experiment guides covering disciplines such as weather/meteorology (cloud identification, temperature measurement, wind observation), ecology (plant or invertebrate transects, soil observation, water quality by observation), and naked-eye astronomy (moon phase tracking, constellation observation, shadow length / sundial). Each guide must carry a full safety block with outdoor-specific hazards (sun/UV, heat, terrain, environmental) and pass the same dual-review and dry-run process as indoor guides.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 4 outdoor/field-science guides are authored spanning at least 2 of the 3 topic areas (weather, ecology, naked-eye astronomy)

Each guide carries a complete safety block including outdoor-specific hazards: sun/UV exposure, heat/dehydration, terrain/footing, insect or plant contact where relevant, weather dependency, and required adult supervision for any outdoor field activity
No guide requires specialized lab equipment; all required items are household or freely available (measuring tape, paper, pencil, containers, etc.)
Each guide passes the CI exclusion check; no guide includes activities on the hard exclusion list (e.g. open flame, chemical use outdoors, high-voltage, etc.)
Every claim, measurement technique, and identification criterion is cited to an open/PD/CC source with retrieval date and license
Each guide is authored, science-reviewed, and credentialed-safety-approved by distinct people; a PR-tied review-log entry records roles, sources checked, exclusion-check result, and dry-run result
Each guide is physically dry-run by a second educator in an outdoor setting to confirm timing, safety, and that the procedure works as written under realistic conditions
Guides render correctly in the static site with clean print output; outdoor-specific safety callouts are visually distinct
output: A pull request adding at least 4 outdoor/field-science guides (spanning weather, ecology, or naked-eye astronomy), each with outdoor-specific safety blocks, full citations, dual review, and dry-run results, to the oer-science-labs repository.
